ucs.title: [LLM] Mustard Tempest (TA569 / SocGholish) JS payload spawning host-profile recon LOLBins
ucs.description: Hunts the signature SocGholish post-execution behaviour: a fake browser-update .js launched under wscript.exe from a user-writable directory rapidly spawning the small, fixed bundle of recon binaries (whoami /all, nltest /domain_trusts, net group 'domain admins' /domain, systeminfo) that TA569 uses to profile the host before brokering access to Indrik Spider / LockBit operators.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as recon_cmds dc(Processes.process_name) as recon_distinct from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=wscript.exe AND Processes.parent_process="*.js*" AND (Processes.parent_process="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.parent_process="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.parent_process="*\\Users\\Public\\*") AND Processes.process_name IN (whoami.exe, nltest.exe, net.exe, net1.exe, systeminfo.exe, ipconfig.exe, arp.exe, tasklist.exe, quser.exe, wmic.exe) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_id Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where recon_distinct >= 3 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
// Mustard Tempest / TA569 SocGholish — JS-driven host profiling fan-out
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _recon_bins = dynamic(["whoami.exe","nltest.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","arp.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","wmic.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                                  // exclude machine accounts
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "wscript.exe"                 // SocGholish loader is .js via wscript
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (".js", ".jse")
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\")
| where FileName in~ (_recon_bins)                                 // recon child
| where (FileName =~ "whoami.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/all","/groups","/upn"))
     or (FileName =~ "nltest.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/domain_trusts","/dclist"))
     or (FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("group","localgroup","view","config"))
     or (FileName =~ "systeminfo.exe")
     or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("computersystem","qfe","product"))
     or (FileName in~ ("ipconfig.exe","arp.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe"))
| summarize FirstRecon = min(Timestamp),
            LastRecon  = max(Timestamp),
            ReconBins  = make_set(FileName),
            ReconCmds  = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 10),
            ReconDistinct = dcount(FileName)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, JsLauncher = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessId
| where ReconDistinct >= 3                                         // fan-out, not a one-off whoami
| where datetime_diff('second', LastRecon, FirstRecon) <= 300       // tight burst (≤5 min) — operator template
| order by FirstRecon desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Mustard Tempest / TA569 SocGholish — JS-driven host profiling fan-out (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _recon_bins = dynamic(["whoami.exe","nltest.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","arp.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","wmic.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName endswith @"\wscript.exe"
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in~ (_recon_bins)
| where (ChildBin =~ "whoami.exe"   and CommandLine has_any ("/all","/groups","/upn"))
     or (ChildBin =~ "nltest.exe"   and CommandLine has_any ("/domain_trusts","/dclist"))
     or (ChildBin in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe") and CommandLine has_any ("group","localgroup","view","config"))
     or (ChildBin =~ "systeminfo.exe")
     or (ChildBin =~ "wmic.exe"     and CommandLine has_any ("computersystem","qfe","product"))
     or (ChildBin in~ ("ipconfig.exe","arp.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe"))
| summarize FirstRecon = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastRecon  = max(TimeGenerated),
            ReconBins  = make_set(ChildBin),
            ReconCmds  = make_set(CommandLine, 10),
            ReconDistinct = dcount(ChildBin)
            by Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, ProcessId
| where ReconDistinct >= 3
| where datetime_diff('second', LastRecon, FirstRecon) <= 300
| order by FirstRecon desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: SocGholish JS Payload Spawning Reconnaissance LOLBins (Mustard Tempest / TA569)
id: 8a4c9f2e-1d6b-4a5c-9e7f-mt1020socgholish
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the signature Mustard Tempest (TA569 / SocGholish / DEV-0206 / GOLD PRELUDE) post-payload behaviour
  in which a fake browser-update .js executed via wscript.exe rapidly spawns multiple host-profiling LOLBins
  (whoami /all, nltest /domain_trusts, net group, systeminfo). This bundle is sent back to the operator before
  hand-off to Indrik Spider for follow-on ransomware (LockBit, WastedLocker).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1020/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/04/04/
  - https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/threats/socgholish/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.007
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1082
  - attack.t1087.002
  - attack.t1482
  - detection.threat_hunting
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  parent_wscript:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\wscript.exe'
  recon_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\arp.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
  recon_args:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/all'
      - '/domain_trusts'
      - 'domain admins'
      - 'localgroup administrators'
      - 'computersystem'
  condition: parent_wscript and (recon_child or recon_args)
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate enterprise login/logoff scripts that genuinely invoke whoami / net / nltest from wscript
  - Software-asset inventory scripts written in JScript
level: high
ucs.rationale: wscript.exe is rarely a parent of a tightly-clustered burst of >=3 different recon LOLBins in modern enterprises — this is the canonical TA569/Mustard Tempest 'host profile' that has been documented by Microsoft (Mustard Tempest), CrowdStrike (GOLD PRELUDE), Mandiant (UNC1543), Red Canary and Proofpoint (TA569) since ~2017. The .js-from-Downloads/Temp constraint plus the recon flag set (/all, /dom
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Mustard Tempest fake-update ZIP-from-browser → wscript JS execution chain
ucs.description: Earlier-stage hunt for the Mustard Tempest (SocGholish) initial-access pattern: a browser drops a ZIP into Downloads/Temp from a compromised legitimate site (drive-by + fake browser/Chrome/Edge update lure) followed within 30 minutes by wscript.exe executing a .js from the same user-writable path — catches the chain before the recon fan-out happens.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as drop_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.zip" AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") AND Filesystem.process_name IN (chrome.exe, msedge.exe, firefox.exe, brave.exe, iexplore.exe, opera.exe) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_name as zip_name file_path as zip_path | join type=inner dest user [ | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as exec_time values(Processes.process) as js_cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as launcher from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN (wscript.exe, cscript.exe) AND (Processes.process="*.js*" OR Processes.process="*.jse*") AND (Processes.process="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Users\\Public\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` ] | where exec_time >= drop_time AND exec_time <= drop_time+1800 | eval delay_sec = exec_time - drop_time | convert ctime(drop_time) ctime(exec_time) | sort - exec_time
ucs.kql:
// Mustard Tempest / SocGholish fake-update ZIP-from-browser → wscript JS launch (≤30 min)
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","arc.exe"]);
let _drops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_browsers)
    | where FileName endswith ".zip"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              Account = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              ZipName = FileName, ZipPath = FolderPath,
              FileOriginUrl, FileOriginReferrerUrl;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".js", ".jse")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("services.exe","msiexec.exe")    // exclude installers / scheduled-task service
| join kind=inner _drops on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (DropTime .. DropTime + 30m)
| project DropTime, ExecTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, DropTime),
          DeviceName, Account,
          ZipName, ZipPath, ZipOriginUrl = FileOriginUrl, ZipReferrer = FileOriginReferrerUrl,
          JsCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          Launcher = InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by ExecTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Mustard Tempest / SocGholish fake-update ZIP-from-browser → wscript JS launch (Sentinel ASIM)
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe"]);
let _drops =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where ActingProcessName has_any (_browsers)
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".zip"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              Account = ActorUsername,
              ZipName = TargetFileName, ZipPath = TargetFilePath;
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where TargetProcessName has_any ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (".js", ".jse")
| where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\")
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner _drops on $left.DvcHostname == $right.DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (DropTime .. DropTime + 30m)
| project DropTime, ExecTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, DropTime),
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ZipName, ZipPath,
          JsCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine,
          Launcher = ParentProcessName
| order by ExecTime desc
ucs.rationale: The browser-dropped ZIP into Downloads/Temp followed by wscript.exe running .js from the same path within 30 minutes is the textbook SocGholish (Mustard Tempest / TA569) drive-by chain — fake Chrome/Edge/Firefox update banners served from compromised WordPress sites have been the group's exclusive delivery vehicle since 2017. Generic JS-from-browser-ZIP is rare in modern enterprises (legitimate ap
